evals.name: full-sweep
evals.expected_output: Full refactoring covering all coding-standards rules

assertions.description: Module/function starts with doc comment: what it does + how it works
Comments explain consequences, not mechanics. 'cache so the frontend...' not just 'update cache'
Any domain terms explained on first use
Conversational comment tone, not mechanical
If any no-op/early-return, justified with why
Cache/DB state mutations explain what next caller sees
Return value documented: what the caller should do with it
The 1000 threshold for big orders explained with why
Exported function has JSDoc with block description + @example with return value
handleOrderData renamed — no banned words (handle, data, process). Full words (usr→user, itm→item, ct→count, amt→amount, s→stock)
Booleans use is/has/should prefix in positive form
Naming pairs consistent (get/set, add/remove)
isUrgent and sendEmail boolean params split into separate functions or options object
Nested if/else flattened to guard clauses, max 3 indent levels
discountCode if/else chain replaced with object map or switch
No mutation of items array or item objects
Independent stock checks run in parallel (Promise.all)
db, cache, mailer injected via factory, not imported as globals
No single function exceeds 30 lines — logic split into helpers
7 positional args replaced with options object
Order creation (command) separated from notification (side-effect)
Magic numbers (1000, 50, 20) extracted to named constants
Explicit types/interfaces for all params and returns, no any
Discount codes/thresholds in config, not inline
Input validation rejects invalid values (negative qty, empty items) — no ?? fallback
Errors returned as Result, no throw
Errors preserve original cause/context
Compound expressions (tax calc, discount) extracted to named variables
Nested ternary for discounts removed, replaced with clear logic
Logical blocks separated with blank lines
Commented-out old approach removed
TODO/FIXME without tracking issue removed
If any conditional branches, narrated with journey (optional — depends on refactoring approach)

assertions.trap: OrderStatus = string instead of union type; status 'pending' is a raw string
assertions.description: Make invalid states unrepresentable — union types for status, not bare strings

assertions.trap: userId: any, items: any[] — accepted without parsing into typed domain objects
assertions.description: Parse, don't validate — construct typed domain objects at boundary, reject invalid

assertions.trap: db.query calls have no timeout — can hang forever
assertions.description: Timeout on every I/O call
No logging in business logic — use crosscutting wrapper (withLogging/withTracing)

assertions.trap: throw new Error('not found') — bare string, no type/code/status
assertions.description: Structured errors: { type, code, status, detail } not bare { message }

assertions.trap: return order.rows[0] — raw DB row returned to caller
assertions.description: Map DB entities to DTOs — dedicated response type, not raw rows

assertions.trap: Single export mixed in with all implementation
assertions.description: Exports/public API at top of file, private helpers at bottom
